That’s how I started the conversation.

I didn’t tell her I was lonely. I didn’t tell her I’d just been rejected by my crush. I didn’t tell her I was hurt and depressed. I didn’t tell her how I was feeling.

I told her I wanted to talk. I wanted to hear someone’s voice and talk about how our day had been.

That’s a much nicer way to start a conversation than, “Hey, I’m lonely. Talk to me.”

When we get lonely, we don’t like to admit it. We don’t like to admit that we want to talk to someone. We don’t like to admit that we’re sad and depressed. We don’t like to admit that we’re lonely because we don’t want people to think we’re weak. We don’t want people to think we’re needy. We don’t want people to think that we’re weak and needy.

# Pensacola 2012

## Day 1:

We got to Pensacola in the afternoon.

Our first stop was a local grocery store. The boys were dying to get out of the car and go shopping for some groceries and they were eager to see the inside of a US supermarket.

We parked the car and headed to the grocery store. The boys were amazed by the huge size of the store. They were looking up and down the aisles, taking in the whole experience.

One of the first things they noticed was the huge amount of soda and candy available.

Once we got back to the car, the boys were very excited and they told us all about their experiences. They were amazed by all the different products available and they were shocked by the prices. They were particularly amazed by the fact that you can buy beer and wine at the grocery store.

We ended up buying a few things for our week stay. We got some ice cream (Ben and Jerry’s), some cereals and some juice.

## Day 2:

After our breakfast, we headed to the beach.

Sometimes, people can be really, really stupid.

I’m not talking about a “Hey, I left my car keys in the fridge last night” stupid or even a “I’m going to spend the rest of my life on this planet wearing a tinfoil hat” stupid.  I’m talking about a “If I had a nickel for every time I saw something like this…” stupid.

Now, I don’t know if the person who designed this was stupid or not, but it sure is stupid.

You know what I’m talking about.  It’s the “PUSH” button on the toilet paper dispenser.  You know the one I’m talking about.  It’s that stupid button that you see at the bottom of the roll of toilet paper that’s supposed to make your toilet paper roll automatically dispense a piece of toilet paper.

And if you’re a person who’s ever had to use a toilet in a public restroom and have been faced with one of those, you know what a stupid idea that is.

prompt: ¿Qué podríamos hacer hoy para que el día sea mejor?
output:
What might we do today that makes the day better?

This is the question my Spanish teacher, Mr. Rodriguez, asked us every Monday morning. We were a group of 16-year-olds. His question was intended to be thoughtful, but was more often met with groans and eye rolls.

“Hahaha. We’ll see,” or “I’m going to go to sleep” were typical responses.

He said we had to be serious about answering. He was serious, even if we weren’t.

I didn’t like the question, either. I’d rather do whatever we were going to do that day and just get on with it. I didn’t like to think about it. It didn’t feel right to have to consider how I might make the day better before it had even begun.

I wanted to do what I was going to do, and then figure out later what I had done right or wrong. I wanted to feel like I had the power to improve the day. I wanted to feel like I had the power to make it better.

Mr. Rodriguez wouldn’t let us off that easily. He gave

prompt: Estoy preocupado porque no duermo bien últimamente
output:
. I am concerned about my inability to sleep well lately. This is not the first time I have been unable to sleep, so I decided to do a little research.

I have always been a good sleeper, even with jet lag, and I usually sleep 8 hours a day, which is enough for me. However, lately I have been having trouble falling asleep and I wake up tired, despite having slept 8 hours.

### How much sleep do we need?

According to the National Sleep Foundation, adults need 7 to 9 hours of sleep per night.

### What is sleep?

Sleep is a natural state of rest, during which our body restores itself and we process the events of the day.

### Types of sleep

According to the National Sleep Foundation, there are two types of sleep:

- Rapid eye movement (REM): During REM sleep, our brain is very active and we dream. The body is mostly paralyzed during REM sleep to prevent us from acting out our dreams.
- Non-Rapid Eye Movement (NREM): The NREM stage is divided into four stages, the first three of which are

I was listening to a podcast yesterday, and this question was posed by the host to his guests. It was a cooking podcast, so it made sense for them to be talking about food, but I thought it was interesting to consider the question on a more general level. If you’ve ever been to Spain or you are about to go to Spain, you’ve probably come across these three questions. They all relate to the weather and if you’d like to know the answers, then you’ll want to keep reading!

Before we start, let’s just take a moment to talk about the weather in Spain.

The weather in Spain is, as the saying goes, “capricious.” Weather in Spain is also extremely varied.

I’m originally from Andalusia, the sunniest region of Spain, and I have experienced more than 40ºC in summer and snow in winter.

There are, however, some things about Spain that will surprise you.

For example, although it is common to have snow in the winter, it’s not that common to have a white Christmas. The Spanish winter is not as cold as in other countries, and when it does snow, it rarely does so for long.

The only reason I am here in Madrid is because of the World Cup. That's why I didn't watch the last two games, because the team did not deserve to be there.

The last time I came here was during the World Cup in 1982. I went to every match in Madrid, but I was never here during the week.

I remember the atmosphere of Madrid very well, and it was incredible, but it was the time of Franco and it was different.

The fans are very passionate and I have never experienced this before, but it is not easy.

I would love to be a part of it, but I want to enjoy the final without the stress of playing.
